nodes.context: International motorcycle gang involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and violent crimes; active in Denmark with ten chapters.
International motorcycle gang; rival of Bandidos in Nordic Biker War and subsequent conflicts.
Criminal organization in Denmark; linked to Bandidos.
Forerunner to Bandidos in Denmark; initially a support club of Hells Angels.
Rebranded from Morticians; merged with clubs opposed to Hells Angels; later patched over by Bandidos.
Motorcycle club in southern Sweden; aligned with Bandidos during Nordic Biker War.
Support group of Hells Angels; suspected in truck attack on Bandidos property in 2012.
Immigrant gang in Denmark; seized markets from biker gangs.
Immigrant gang in Denmark; seized markets from biker gangs.
Immigrant gang in Denmark; seized markets from biker gangs.
Immigrant gang in Denmark; seized markets from biker gangs.
Immigrant gang in Denmark; seized markets from biker gangs.
Dutch motorcycle club; opened chapter in Denmark in 2013.
German motorcycle gang; expanded into Denmark.
German motorcycle gang; expanded into Denmark.
German motorcycle gang; expanded into Denmark.
Motorcycle gang involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and violent crimes in Denmark.
Criminal gang in Køge, Denmark.
Gang involved in conflict with Bandidos in 2013.
Breakaway group from Bandidos' Westside chapter in Næstved, active 2013-2014.
Motorcycle gang in Denmark, formed from former Westside Nation members.
Dominant motorcycle gang in Denmark, entered agreement with Bandidos and Satudarah in 2019.
Gang in Næstved, involved in feud with Bandidos over hash market.
Immigrant gang in Helsingør, involved in conflict with Bandidos in 2015.
Gang whose members were murdered by Bandidos in 2015.
Motorcycle gang in North Zealand, involved in turf war with Bandidos.
Gang patched over by Gremium in 2017.

edges.evidence_quote: The club has links to the Turkish mafia in Denmark.
edges.context: Bandidos have links to Turkish mafia in Denmark.

edges.evidence_quote: The Morticians were initially a support club of the Hells Angels
edges.context: Morticians were a support club of Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: the Undertakers were patched over by the Bandidos on 17 December 1993.
edges.context: Undertakers merged into Bandidos as a chapter.

edges.evidence_quote: the Morbids aligned with the Bandidos
edges.context: Morbids aligned with Bandidos during Nordic Biker War.

edges.evidence_quote: sparking a three-year-long gang war between the clubs for control over the drug trade in the Nordic countries.
edges.context: Bandidos and Hells Angels fought a war for control of drug trade.

edges.evidence_quote: the clubs agreed to cease expansion in the Nordic countries and to divide territories of criminal activity.
edges.context: Bandidos and Hells Angels signed a peace agreement ending the war.

edges.evidence_quote: Renewed tensions between the Bandidos and Hells Angels resulted in an outbreak of violence in September 2012.
edges.context: Renewed tensions led to violence in 2012.

edges.evidence_quote: Police suspect the incident was carried out by the Hells Angels support group AK81.
edges.context: AK81 is a support group of Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: A war between the Bandidos and the Black Cobra gang erupted in Køge after Oruc Türkoglu, a leading Black Cobra figure in the city, was wounded in a stabbing by Bandidos members on 29 January 2013.
edges.context: Bandidos and Black Cobra were in a violent conflict in 2013.

edges.evidence_quote: A truce was reached and the conflict was brought to an end after four representatives from each gang met on 29 March 2013.
edges.context: Bandidos and Black Cobra agreed to a truce ending their conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Bandidos sergeant-at-arms Peter Buch Rosenberg and another club member, Michael Fuhlendorff, were convicted of weapons possession after police discovered two loaded guns, a silencer, binoculars and other equipment at a cottage in Dronningmølle where Rosenberg was living, protected by two bodyguards, during a conflict with the Brothas Souljaz gang.
edges.context: Bandidos were in a conflict with Brothas Souljaz in 2013.

edges.evidence_quote: In July 2013, a conflict erupted between the Bandidos and the Westside Nation, a breakaway group of former members of the club's "Westside" chapter in Næstved who defected following a dispute over the club's national leadership.
edges.context: Bandidos and Westside Nation were in a conflict starting July 2013.

edges.evidence_quote: The Westside Nation disbanded in 2014, with some members returning to the Bandidos and others patching over to Satudarah, forming the core of the club's Copenhagen chapter.
edges.context: Some Westside Nation members joined Satudarah after disbanding.

edges.evidence_quote: This resulted in the beginning of a gang war between the Bandidos and Satudarah.
edges.context: Bandidos and Satudarah engaged in a gang war after Westside Nation members joined Satudarah.

edges.evidence_quote: Denmark's three dominant motorcycle gangs – the Bandidos, Hells Angels and Satudarah – reportedly entered into an agreement to counteract disputes in June 2019.
edges.context: Bandidos, Hells Angels, and Satudarah made an agreement to reduce conflicts.

edges.evidence_quote: Denmark's three dominant motorcycle gangs – the Bandidos, Hells Angels and Satudarah – reportedly entered into an agreement to counteract disputes in June 2019.
edges.context: Satudarah and Hells Angels were part of a tripartite agreement with Bandidos.

edges.evidence_quote: Beginning in 2014, a feud involving the Bandidos and the Black Army resulted in numerous shootings and assaults in Næstved, where both gangs have clubhouses.
edges.context: Bandidos and Black Army were in a feud over the hash market from 2014.

edges.evidence_quote: An attack on a man in the Vapnagård housing estate in Helsingør on 30 June 2015 was linked to a conflict between the Bandidos and the immigrant gang LTF.
edges.context: Bandidos and LTF were in a conflict in 2015.

edges.evidence_quote: A Bandidos member holding the rank of krigsminister (“minister for war”) within the club was convicted of the 11 November 2015 triple murder of Philip Rasmussen, Suhaib Jaffar, and Mike Patrick Winther, members of a gang known as the Vanløse Group who were killed with a revolver and a shotgun as they slept in their apartment in Frederiksberg, Copenhagen.
edges.context: Bandidos members murdered members of the Vanløse Group in 2015.

edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos have also been involved in a turf war with Gremium in North Zealand after Gremium opened a chapter in the traditional Bandidos stronghold of Helsinge on 3 February 2017 by patching-over the Local BrotherHood (LBH) gang.
edges.context: Bandidos and Gremium were in a turf war starting in 2017.

edges.evidence_quote: Gremium opened a chapter in the traditional Bandidos stronghold of Helsinge on 3 February 2017 by patching-over the Local BrotherHood (LBH) gang.
edges.context: Gremium absorbed Local BrotherHood by patching them over.
